bullets.text: At least six people were killed in Kyiv after a gunman opened fire and took hostages inside a supermarket, with the attacker subsequently killed in a shoot-out with police.
More than £1bn has been pledged for Sudan at a conference in Berlin, exceeding the funding target for the war-ravaged country as its humanitarian crisis deepens.
The Swiss National Bank has cut interest rates by a half point to 0.5%, with the dollar jumping 0.5% against the franc.
Iran has reportedly opened fire on two Indian-flagged ships in the Strait of Hormuz, just one day after the foreign minister declared the strait open.
The prospect of sea mines in the Strait of Hormuz highlights a gap in US naval capabilities, reinforcing the need for NATO assistance.
Suspiciously timed bets totaling over $1 billion on the Iran war are raising concerns among lawmakers, suggesting potential insider trading.
Iran has asserted 'strict control' over the Strait of Hormuz, indicating it will not be fully reopened, leading to warnings from Donald Trump against 'blackmailing' Washington.
The conflict in the Middle East is expected to revive stagflation dangers for the global economy, with cumulative impacts emerging in business surveys.
Economic pain from the Iran war is projected to disproportionately affect poor countries, with warnings at IMF/World Bank meetings that some developing nations may require additional lending.
